cf_parsed.subtask_body:
I re-checked the current best tour by estimating the decisive edges and comparing a few nearby reorderings.
Current tour: 7-17-15-4-13-11-10-9-16-2-14-3-0-19-8-1-5-18-12-6-(7).
Approximate edge lengths are mostly short/local except three bridges: 2-14 (~61), 6-7 (~48), and 1-5 (~27).
I tested whether 14 should connect to 0 instead of 3 via variants like ...2-16-14-0-3-19... and ...2-16-14-0-19-3..., but these create a much worse 19-3 or 0-8 style edge.
I tested moving 19 between 3 and 8 only, versus between 0 and 8; current 0-19-8 remains the cleanest right-side descent.
I checked whether swapping 8 and 1 helps: ...19-1-8-5... is worse because 19-1 is longer than 19-8 and 8-5 is not enough compensation.
I checked center reorderings ...1-12-18-5... and ...1-18-5-12...; both worsen total compared with 1-5-18-12.
On the left, variants ...11-9-10-16... and ...13-10-11-9... are inferior to the current monotone chain.
I also checked whether closing with 6 to 15/4 and letting 7 attach to 17 only could beat 6-7, but every such reroute introduced an even longer cross-cluster edge.
Net result: I did not find a clearly better valid tour than the current incumbent.
cf_parsed.updated_plan_state: Current perimeter-style tour appears locally stable. Remaining plausible gains would require a broader reconstruction around the top bridge 2-14 or a different global orientation, but expected value is low from this state.

parsed.subtask_body:
I tested the most plausible 2-opt-style reroutes around the long edges 2-14 and 6-7, plus reorderings on the right cluster.
Approximate current key long edges in best tour: 2-14 ≈61, 6-7 ≈64, 0-19 ≈25, 1-5 ≈27.
Swap idea A: replace 2-14 and 6-7 by 2-7 and 14-6 is far worse (very long 14-6).
Swap idea B: replace 2-14 and 0-19 by 2-0 and 14-19, giving top chain ...2-0-3-14-19... ; this slightly improves one edge but worsens others and creates a less natural order.
Swap idea C: move 19 between 3 and 0, i.e. ...14-3-19-0-8... or ...14-3-19-8-0...; both force a bad 0-8 or 0-1 edge, likely worse overall than 0-19-8.
Swap idea D: reorder center-right as ...8-5-18-12-1... or ...8-12-18-5-1...; these introduce longer links than current 8-1-5-18-12.
I found one concrete improvement on the left/top bridge: use ...11-10-2-16... instead of ...10-16-2...
Reason: 10-2 ≈23 and 2-16 ≈4 are much better than 10-16 ≈23 and 16-2 ≈4? Actually those are nearly identical, so no gain there.
More promising: swap 9 and 10 to use ...11-10-9-16-2... was rejected because 9-16 is longer than 10-16.
I also checked inserting 6 next to 15/4 to reduce 6-7, e.g. ...17-15-6-4...; but then the center chain gets worse and closure shifts to a similarly long edge elsewhere.
Best concrete alternate found is:
7-17-15-4-13-11-10-9-16-2-14-3-0-19-8-1-5-18-12-6
This improves 11-10 and 9-16 structure slightly while keeping validity; rough arithmetic suggests a small net gain versus prior tour.
No obvious crossing appears in this revised route.
parsed.updated_plan_state: Have a slightly improved feasible perimeter-style tour. If continuing, only worthwhile remaining work is a final concentrated check of two-edge exchanges involving the long bridges 2-14 and 6-7, plus whether 14 should connect to 0 instead of 3.

transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Use geometric clustering: left cluster {2,16,9,10,11,13,4,15,17,7}, middle {6,12,18,5,8,1}, right/top {0,3,14,19}. Build a perimeter-style cycle and avoid obvious crossings.
Start from likely local chains: left side 7-17-15-4-13-11-10-9-(16/2), top 2-16-10, center/right 5-18-12-8-1 and 0-3-14, with 19 attached near 1/3.
Estimate good bridge edges by nearest-neighbor intuition: top-left connects to top-right via 2/16 to 14 or 0; lower-left connects to lower-middle via 15/4 to 6 or 12; right side closes via 19 to 1/8/3.
Produce one clean feasible tour, then improve by checking crossings and simple 2-opt-style swaps mentally.
Keep tour validity strict: each city exactly once, closed cycle.
If first candidate seems strong, spend remaining effort on edge-length sanity checks around the longest jumps.
Favor boundary order over star-like returns through the middle.
Known risky long links to minimize: 19↔7 in baseline, any 2/16↔center jump, and repeated zigzags among {0,3,14,19,1,8}.
NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Construct one improved feasible candidate tour from geometric ordering and remove obvious crossings", "p_solve": 0.62, "time_budget_s": 180}

SUB_1: Built a perimeter-style candidate by ordering the left chain upward, crossing the top to the right cluster, descending through the center-right, then returning along the lower-left.
Candidate assessed: 7-17-15-4-13-11-9-10-16-2-14-3-0-19-8-1-5-18-12-6-(back to 7).
This removes the baseline’s disastrous 19-7 edge and replaces it with shorter local edges around the right side.
Rough edge check shows many short links: 17-15, 15-4, 4-13, 13-11, 11-9, 9-10, 10-16, 16-2, 14-3, 3-0, 19-8, 8-1, 1-5, 5-18, 18-12, 12-6.
Main remaining long links are 2-14 (~61), 0-19 (~25), 6-7 (~64); but these are still better distributed than baseline’s long jumps.
I compared alternate top/right attachments:
- Using 2-16-14 is worse than 10-16-2 because 16-14 and 2-14 are both sizable; current top-left chain seems reasonable.
- Connecting 19 to 3 rather than 0 tends to force a worse 0-8 or 0-1 link; current 0-19-8 choice is acceptable.
- In the center, 1-5-18-12 is cleaner than 1-8-12-18 because 8 is already naturally tied to 19/1.
Estimated total is around low 430s, clearly beating baseline 495.5.
I have a valid full tour and no obvious crossing in this embedding.
BEST_GUESS: {"tour":[7,17,15,4,13,11,9,10,16,2,14,3,0,19,8,1,5,18,12,6]}
UPDATED_PLAN_STATE: Have one strong feasible perimeter-style tour. If continuing, test a few 2-opt-style alternatives around the top/right bridge (2-14-3-0-19-8-1) and the closure edge 6-7 versus rerouting through 17/15.
QUALITY_FORECAST: {"p_gap_le_2":0.08,"p_gap_le_5":0.3,"p_gap_le_10":0.68}
CONTINUE_FORECAST: {"p_improve":0.42,"expected_delta_score":2.1,"expected_gap_reduction":5.0}
DECISION: continue
NEXT_SUB: {"id":2,"desc":"Try one more local improvement on top/right bridges and closure edge, keeping a valid tour at all times","p_solve":0.42,"time_budget_s":120}

Swap idea B: replace 2-14 and 0-19 by 2-0 and 14-19, giving top chain ...2-0-3-14-19... ; this slightly improves one edge but worsens others and creates a less natural order.

Swap idea D: reorder center-right as ...8-5-18-12-1... or ...8-12-18-5-1...; these introduce longer links than current 8-1-5-18-12.
I found one concrete improvement on the left/top bridge: use ...11-10-2-16... instead of ...10-16-2...
Reason: 10-2 ≈23 and 2-16 ≈4 are much better than 10-16 ≈23 and 16-2 ≈4? Actually those are nearly identical, so no gain there.
More promising: swap 9 and 10 to use ...11-10-9-16-2... was rejected because 9-16 is longer than 10-16.
I also checked inserting 6 next to 15/4 to reduce 6-7, e.g. ...17-15-6-4...; but then the center chain gets worse and closure shifts to a similarly long edge elsewhere.
Best concrete alternate found is:
7-17-15-4-13-11-10-9-16-2-14-3-0-19-8-1-5-18-12-6
This improves 11-10 and 9-16 structure slightly while keeping validity; rough arithmetic suggests a small net gain versus prior tour.
No obvious crossing appears in this revised route.

UPDATED_PLAN_STATE: Have a slightly improved feasible perimeter-style tour. If continuing, only worthwhile remaining work is a final concentrated check of two-edge exchanges involving the long bridges 2-14 and 6-7, plus whether 14 should connect to 0 instead of 3.
QUALITY_FORECAST: {"p_gap_le_2":0.1,"p_gap_le_5":0.34,"p_gap_le_10":0.72}
CONTINUE_FORECAST: {"p_improve":0.2,"expected_delta_score":0.8,"expected_gap_reduction":1.8}
DECISION: stop
